text:
Pollastro v. Pollastro
Collection
Decisions of the Court of Appeal
Date
1998-11-17
Docket numbers
C30824, M23453
Judges
Charron, Louise Vivianne
Subject
Family
Decision Content
DATE: 19981117 DOCKET: C30824 M23453 COURT OF APPEAL FOR ONTARIO RE: JOHN ANTHONY POLLASTRO (Applicant/Respondent) v. REESA STACEY POLLASTRO (Respondent/Appellant) BEFORE: CHARRON J.A. (In chambers) COUNSEL: Phyllis Brodkin, for the appellant Roselyn Zisman, for the respondent HEARD: November 16, 1998 ENDORSEMENT
[1] The appellant seeks a stay of the order of Beaulieu J. dated October 16, 1998 pending disposition of her appeal. Pursuant to the terms of the Hague Convention, Beaulieu J. ordered the immediate return of the parties' child to California subject to an interim custody order in favour of the appellant for a period of 30 days if she returned with the child. At issue on the appeal is whether Beaulieu J. erred in finding that the appellant had not successfully demonstrated that the exception under Article 13(b) applied (return of child not mandated where there is a grave risk that the return "would expose the child to physical or psychological harm or otherwise place the child in an intolerable situation").
[2] In my view, the application should be granted. The appeal raises a serious issue as to what extent the evidence of harm presented on the application went to the merits of the custody hearing or to establishing the grounds for an exception under Article 13 (b). If the stay is not granted, the appeal will be nugatory and in this sense, the appellant will suffer irreparable harm. On the other hand, the appeal can be heard expeditiously and any further delay can be kept at a minimum. Hence the balance of convenience favours the appellant.
[3] The respondent argues that a more stringent test should be met before a stay is granted given Canada's obligation under the treaty to deal with such matters expeditiously. The nature of the proceeding is obviously a relevant consideration on the stay application. It is my view, however, that the treaty obligation to secure "the prompt return of children" in appropriate cases does not translate into a more stringent test on a stay application. Rather, the obligation should be fulfilled by hearing the appeal as expeditiously as possible.
[4] Therefore, the application is granted. The order of Beaulieu J. is stayed pending the hearing of the appeal. The appeal is ordered expedited. Both parties are forthwith directed to seek a date for hearing on an expedited basis from the Registrar of this Court or his delegate.
November 16, 1998 Charron J.A.
